request.question: Test FK506 (10nM) on T-cell IL-2 secretion in Jurkat cells, ELISA readout.

references.title: Delta-opioid receptors expressed by Jurkat T cells enhance IL-2 secretion by increasing AP-1 complexes and activity of the NF-AT/AP-1-binding promoter element.
references.relevance_note: This paper discusses the enhancement of IL-2 secretion in Jurkat T cells, which is directly relevant to the measurable outcome of the hypothesis.

references.title: A COX-2 metabolite of the endogenous cannabinoid, 2-arachidonyl glycerol, mediates suppression of IL-2 secretion in activated Jurkat T cells.
references.relevance_note: This study examines the suppression of IL-2 secretion in activated Jurkat T cells, providing insights into factors that may influence IL-2 levels.

references.title: Somatostatin increases mitogen‐induced IL‐2 secretion and proliferation of human jurkat T cells via sst3 receptor isotype
references.relevance_note: This paper investigates the increase of IL-2 secretion in Jurkat T cells, which relates to the cytokine secretion aspect of the hypothesis.

literature_qc.explanation: The intervention FK506 is a known immunosuppressant that affects T cell function, and while it is not explicitly mentioned in the retrieved papers, the biological system of Jurkat cells is covered in several studies (e.g., papers [3], [4], and [5]) that investigate IL-2 secretion in this cell line. The experiment type, focusing on T-cell cytokine secretion assays, is also relevant to the context of these papers, which examine factors influencing IL-2 secretion in Jurkat cells. However, none of the papers directly address FK506's effect on IL-2 secretion in Jurkat cells specifically.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Related publications exist. Review the references, cite relevant prior work in your plan, and explicitly state how your experiment differs or extends the existing approach.

plan.objective: To determine the effect of FK506 (10 nM) on IL-2 secretion in Jurkat cells.
plan.experimental_design: Jurkat cells will be cultured and treated with FK506 (10 nM) for 24 hours. IL-2 secretion will be measured using an ELISA assay. Control groups will include untreated Jurkat cells and cells treated with a vehicle control.
controls: Jurkat cells stimulated with 5 µg/mL PHA (phytohemagglutinin) to induce IL-2 secretion.
Jurkat cells treated with vehicle (DMSO) without FK506.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Cell Culture Preparation
Thaw Jurkat cells from liquid nitrogen storage and transfer to a 15 mL conical tube. Add 10 mL of RPMI 1640 medium supplemented with 10% FBS and 1% penicillin-streptomycin. Centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature. Discard supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 10 mL of fresh RPMI 1640 medium.
Equipment: Eppendorf 5424 centrifuge, 15 mL conical tubes
Reagents: Jurkat cells (ATCC TIB-152), RPMI 1640 medium (Gibco), Fetal Bovine Serum (FBS, Sigma), Penicillin-Streptomycin (Gibco)
Expected outcome: Cell viability >90% as assessed by trypan blue exclusion.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Cell Seeding
Count Jurkat cells using a hemocytometer and adjust the concentration to 1 × 10^6 cells/mL. Seed 1 mL of cell suspension into each well of a 24-well plate (total of 12 wells).
Equipment: Hemocytometer, 24-well tissue culture plate
Expected outcome: Cells evenly distributed at a density of 1 × 10^6 cells/mL in each well.

DAY 1 | 15 min | Treatment with FK506
Prepare FK506 stock solution at 1 mM in DMSO. Dilute to 10 nM in RPMI 1640 medium. Add 1 mL of FK506 solution to 6 wells and 1 mL of DMSO (vehicle control) to 6 wells. Incubate cells at 37°C with 5% CO2 for 24 h.
Equipment: Pipettes, 37°C incubator with 5% CO2
Reagents: FK506 (10 nM), DMSO (Sigma)
Expected outcome: FK506 is successfully added to the treatment wells without affecting cell viability.

DAY 1 | 30 min | IL-2 Secretion Measurement
After 24 h treatment, collect 100 µL of supernatant from each well and transfer to a new 96-well plate. Store at -20°C until analysis. Prepare IL-2 ELISA kit according to the manufacturer's instructions (e.g., eBioscience).
Equipment: 96-well plate, Pipettes
Reagents: IL-2 ELISA kit (eBioscience)
Expected outcome: Supernatant collected without contamination, ready for ELISA analysis.

DAY 1 | 3 h | ELISA Assay
Thaw the collected supernatants and perform the ELISA according to the kit protocol. Add 50 µL of each sample and standard to the respective wells. Incubate for 2 h at room temperature. Wash wells 3 times with wash buffer. Add 100 µL of detection antibody and incubate for 1 h. Wash wells again and add 100 µL of substrate solution. Measure absorbance at 450 nm after 30 min.
Equipment: Microplate reader (BioTek Synergy HT), Wash buffer, Pipettes
Expected outcome: Absorbance readings obtained for all samples, with a standard curve established for IL-2 quantification.

DAY 1 | 1 h | Data Analysis
Calculate IL-2 concentrations from the absorbance values using the standard curve. Compare IL-2 levels between FK506-treated and control groups using a one-way ANOVA followed by Tukey's post-hoc test for multiple comparisons.
Expected outcome: Statistical significance determined with p < 0.05 for differences in IL-2 secretion between groups.

DAY 1 | 1 h | Documentation and Reporting
Compile all data, including cell viability, IL-2 concentrations, and statistical analysis results. Prepare a report summarizing the findings and conclusions of the experiment.
Expected outcome: Comprehensive report generated detailing the experimental results and interpretations.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: FK506 effectively inhibits IL-2 secretion in Jurkat cells at 10 nM concentration.
Jurkat cells are responsive to PHA stimulation for IL-2 secretion.
The ELISA kit used is sensitive enough to detect IL-2 levels in the supernatants.
decision_criteria: Proceed if IL-2 secretion in FK506-treated cells is significantly lower than in control cells (p < 0.05).
Proceed if cell viability remains above 90% after treatment with FK506.
Proceed if the standard curve for IL-2 shows a linear relationship with R² > 0.95.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same batch of Jurkat cells for all experiments.
Standardize the FK506 preparation method and DMSO concentration.
Ensure all reagents are from the same supplier and lot number for consistency.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: >=10 percentage points (pilot fallback) improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To determine the effect of FK506 (10 nM) on IL-2 secretion in Jurkat cells.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm

questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
